chunk_summary: This section discusses dehydration assessment and management in children, including IMNCI ORS plans, IV fluid protocols, and zinc supplementation, emphasizing structured evaluation and reassessment to prevent harm.
learning_objectives: Understand the IMNCI dehydration classification and corresponding treatment plans (A, B, C).
Learn the appropriate use of ORS and IV fluids in children with dehydration.
Recognize the importance of zinc supplementation and early feeding in recovery.
Identify warning signs requiring escalation of care in dehydrated children.
key_facts: Plan A: Home-based ORS after each loose stool (50–100 mL for children <2 years, 100–200 mL for 2–10 years).
Plan B: ORS 75 mL/kg over 4 hours under observation, with adjustments for vomiting.
Plan C: IV fluids (RL or 0.9% NS) 100 mL/kg; infants: 30 mL/kg in 1 hour, 70 mL/kg in 5 hours; older children: 30 mL/kg in 30 minutes, 70 mL/kg in 2.5 hours.
Zinc supplementation: 10 mg/day for 2–6 months, 20 mg/day for older infants and children, for 14 days.
Reassess hydration status after each fluid step to avoid over- or under-resuscitation.
Escalate care for shock, persistent vomiting, blood in stool, altered sensorium, or severe acute malnutrition.
reference_quote: Plan C (severe dehydration) is urgent IV isotonic fluid (RL or 0.9% NS) 100 mL/kg: in infants, 30 mL/kg in 1 hour then 70 mL/kg in 5 hours; in older children, 30 mL/kg in 30 minutes then 70 mL/kg in 2.5 hours, with frequent reassessment.
document_key_topics: IMNCI ORS and fluid management plans
